notes: Every check below was run locally at head 1333e3ca (Node v22.23.1) and its exit code read directly; commands were run individually because `npm run check:all` chains with `;` and its exit code reflects only the last command. This artifact adds plan files only and touches no src/, so the code results carry to the artifact commit. B1 is NOT 'measuring previews': T7-T9 are the cross-repo walk Dave named, they are PENDING, and none of the three can be run from this repo. Nothing here is marked pass on expectation.

items.title: Focused B1 suite passes (gate, guards, isolation, no live beacon)
items.precondition: Node v22.23.1, head 1333e3ca, clean worktree. Node 20 breaks vitest with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM.
items.expected: The B1 suite passes with no live Rybbit beacon fired (global.fetch stubbed).
items.evidence: Exit 0. 'Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 16 passed (16)' at head 1333e3ca. Count verified independently, not copied from the review.
items.notes: The 16, read from the file rather than summarized: gate off provisions nothing; unset gate treated as off; LIVE preview provisions + reports actual state; ONLY the preview domain sent to Rybbit (body keys asserted to be exactly [blockBots, domain, name]); prefers a set hostname over generated; no second site on repeat call; each project its own siteId, tenants never mixed; skips not-found / archived / non-LIVE / no-hostname; defers a custom-domain project; skips org-archived; empty-string hostname falls back to generated; refuses a revoked integration without re-syncing; provisioned:false when no active row results. No-live-beacon is a fixture-wide property, not a named case: vi.stubGlobal("fetch", fetchMock) at line 124, so a run never reaches analytics.getalloro.com.

items.title: TypeScript compiles with no errors
items.evidence: Exit 0. Zero lines of output (0-line log).

items.title: Backend conventions gate passes in strict mode
items.expected: Exit 0 with 0 clear structural backend violations.
items.evidence: Exit 0. 'CLEAR structural violations - backend (size + console + db): 0'. Reported separately: 102 Tier A tenant-scope heuristic hits across pre-existing models - ADVISORY, does not fail --strict, and not introduced by this branch.
items.notes: The `--` passthrough is required; npm silently swallows a bare `--strict`, which would make the run a non-receipt.

items.title: Full backend suite stays green (no regression from B1)
items.expected: The whole suite passes; B1 breaks nothing existing.

items.title: PREVIEW_ANALYTICS_ENABLED defaults OFF and is fail-closed
items.precondition: Read the gate in source at head 1333e3ca; run the gate-off test.
steps: Grep every env template, workflow, and config for PREVIEW_ANALYTICS_ENABLED.
Run the gate-off case in the focused suite.
items.expected: With the env var absent, provisionPreviewAnalytics provisions nothing and returns reason 'gate_disabled'.
items.evidence: Verified in code at service.rybbit.ts:174 - `if (process.env.PREVIEW_ANALYTICS_ENABLED !== "true")` returns `{ enabled:false, provisioned:false, reason:"gate_disabled" }` before any DB read or network call. Fail-closed: an absent var is `undefined !== "true"` -> gate returns. Grep confirms the flag is set in NO env template, workflow, or config in the repo - so the shipped default is absent, i.e. OFF. The gate-off path is also proven by test (T1).
items.notes: Only a deliberate operator action (setting the var to exactly the string "true") can enable it. Per Dave's point 2, it must stay off until T7-T9 pass.

items.title: No SQL migration ships with this branch
items.expected: No migration files; the branch is additive with no schema change.
items.evidence: No migration files in the diff. Changed files are limited to plan docs, 1 test, and 5 src files (route, controller, service, model, queries).
items.notes: Un-provisioning is reversible by clearing the integration row / rybbit_site_id.

items.title: Renderer snippet inspected - confirm it injects for preview hostnames
items.precondition: Requires read access to the separate `website-renderer` repo, which owns HTML serving and script injection.
steps: Read the Rybbit snippet injection condition.
Confirm an active rybbit integration row on a *.sites.getalloro.com preview host actually triggers injection.
items.expected: The renderer injects the Rybbit snippet for a preview hostname once an active integration row exists.
items.notes: CANNOT BE RUN FROM THIS REPO. The injection condition lives in the `website-renderer` repo; a machine-wide search found no checkout of it (only alloro plan folders whose names contain 'renderer'). This repo only stores the siteId and provisions it - it never injects. The spec's own premise that 'the renderer injects, no renderer change needed' is PLAUSIBLE BUT UNPROVEN and is exactly what this item would settle. WHAT SETTLES IT: renderer-repo access, then reading the inject condition. Escalated: queue website-renderer access (also blocks follow-on B1-R).

items.title: No-PII / cookieless audit of the injected snippet
items.precondition: Requires read access to the separate `website-renderer` repo (same blocker as T7).
steps: Read the renderer's injected Rybbit snippet.
Confirm it captures no form-field contents, no patient identifiers, and no sensitive query strings in tracked URLs.
items.expected: The snippet is cookieless and emits no PII - the named gate that must pass before PREVIEW_ANALYTICS_ENABLED is set on.
items.notes: CANNOT BE RUN FROM THIS REPO - the snippet is not in this codebase. Rybbit's cookieless posture is ASSUMED FROM PRODUCT DESIGN, NOT VERIFIED; it must be treated as unverified until the snippet is read. This matters because B1 is the first lever to observe real patients on live healthcare-adjacent sites. What B1 itself sends is only the domain string (${host}.sites.getalloro.com) - it adds no PII surface of its own - but that is a different claim from 'the event stream carries no PII', which is what this item settles and which remains OPEN. WHAT SETTLES IT: renderer-repo access, then a read of the snippet. Explicitly NOT waived.

items.title: Live preview observation - one real preview proves clean data flows
items.precondition: Requires T7 + T8 to pass first, then Dave enabling the flag for exactly one project on a deployed environment.
steps: After T7/T8 pass, have Dave set PREVIEW_ANALYTICS_ENABLED=true for one project.
Load that project's *.sites.getalloro.com URL as a customer would.
Confirm the Rybbit snippet is present in the served HTML.
Confirm pageview/session data lands for that siteId.
Confirm the captured payload contains no form values, no patient identifiers, and no sensitive query strings.
items.expected: The active integration row produces pageview/session data with no PII - the point at which B1 may be described as measuring previews.
items.notes: CANNOT BE RUN NOW. Three reasons, each independent: the flag is off by design (T5); there is no deployed preview with this branch's code to observe; and the pre-req snippet audit (T7/T8) has not run. Until this item passes, B1 is 'provisions correctly', NOT 'measures previews' - the spec hero was corrected in Rev 3 to stop claiming the latter. THIS IS THE TRUE FINISH LINE for B1 and it is a POST-MERGE residual. Not waived, not expected-to-pass, not inferred.
